original_reasoning: Arabic soft earth receiving the locust's tail preserves a clear notion of insertion and being fixed in place, close to holding.
blsm variant of balsam; still semitic root. Strong link.
Arabic explicitly means place, location, or valley-side, which aligns well with a named mountain landmark.
Arabic خشم nose/snout and Greek spear-points share the concrete notion of projecting front tips.
Arabic 'intensify/be severe' aligns well with Greek 'terror' as intensified fear.
خثي refers to dunging or excrement, a direct material-to-ground relation with χθονί 'earth/ground'.
Claws or beast weapons directly rend apart, matching asunder on the result side.
The Arabic gloss centers on a measure and measuring vessel, while καλκουλάτωρ is an accountant; both belong to reckoning and quantified handling.
